metadata.title: AI & LLM Models Timeline
metadata.description: Comprehensive list of AI and LLM models from 2017 to 2026, including text, vision, audio, multimodal and image generation models.

models.description: The seminal 'Attention Is All You Need' paper introduced the Transformer architecture, replacing recurrence with self-attention. This became the foundation of virtually all modern LLMs.
OpenAI's first Generative Pre-trained Transformer. Demonstrated that language models could perform various tasks after unsupervised pre-training on diverse text data, then fine-tuning.
Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. Processes text bidirectionally, setting new benchmarks across NLP tasks. Became the foundation for Google Search improvements.
GPT-2 generated such coherent text that OpenAI initially delayed its full public release due to misuse concerns. With 1.5B parameters, it demonstrated emergent text generation capabilities at scale.
notable_features: delayed release due to safety concerns

models.description: NVIDIA's Megatron-LM introduced tensor model parallelism for training multi-billion parameter models efficiently across multiple GPUs. Paved the way for modern distributed training frameworks.
Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer reframed all NLP tasks as text-to-text problems, enabling a unified training objective. Highly influential in fine-tuning and transfer learning research.
GPT-3 with 175B parameters was a landmark model demonstrating remarkable few-shot learning. It showed emergent behaviors that surprised researchers and sparked the modern LLM era.
Solved the 50-year-old protein folding grand challenge, predicting structure from sequence.
GPT-Neo was EleutherAI's open-source alternative to GPT-3. Trained on The Pile (800GB diverse dataset), it democratized access to large-scale language model research.

PanGu-Alpha is a Chinese-language LLM jointly developed by Huawei, Pengcheng Laboratory, and BAAI. One of the first large Chinese LLMs, trained entirely on Chinese text. Introduced a novel Query Layer for better generalization.

Language Model for Dialogue Applications, trained specifically on dialogue data. Gained public attention in 2022 when a Google engineer claimed it showed signs of sentience. It later powered Google's Bard chatbot.

Jurassic-1 from Israel's AI21 Labs was one of the first GPT-3 competitors, at 178B parameters. Available via API with Jumbo (178B) and Large (7.5B) variants. Used a 250,000-token vocabulary — the largest at the time.
A Microsoft + NVIDIA collaboration. MT-NLG 530B was the world's largest dense transformer language model at release. Combined DeepSpeed and Megatron model parallelism techniques.
DeepMind's Gopher was a 280B parameter model trained on MassiveText dataset. It advanced understanding of scaling laws and served as a baseline for the influential Chinchilla research.
Generalist Language Model used a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture to efficiently scale to 1.2T parameters while only activating a fraction per token, demonstrating efficient scaling.

ERNIE 3.0 Titan is Baidu's 260B parameter knowledge-enhanced pre-trained model. It integrates a massive knowledge graph directly into the training process, enabling better factual accuracy and reasoning over structured knowledge. Predecessor to ERNIE Bot.
Chinchilla challenged 'bigger is better' by showing 70B parameters trained on 4x more data outperformed Gopher 280B. Its 'Chinchilla scaling laws' reshaped how the entire industry trains LLMs.
Pathways Language Model used Google's Pathways system to train across thousands of chips simultaneously. Excelled at multi-step reasoning and achieved state-of-the-art on many benchmarks at release.

Flamingo was a pioneering vision-language model that could take interleaved text and images as input. Demonstrated strong few-shot visual understanding and became a blueprint for future multimodal models.

GLM-130B is an open bilingual (Chinese-English) pre-trained model from Tsinghua University. Uses a novel GLM architecture with autoregressive blank-filling instead of standard decoder-only structure. Competitive with GPT-3 on both languages.
First major public release of Stable Diffusion, enabling consumer laptops to generate photorealistic images.

Whisper is a general-purpose speech recognition model trained on 680,000 hours of multilingual audio. Transcribes and translates speech across 99 languages with high accuracy, even in noisy conditions.
Arguably the most influential open-source image model, refined for better portrait quality.
Completely new AI architecture that redefined photorealistic AI art.
BigScience Large Open-Science Open-Access Multilingual model. Created by 1,000+ researchers from 70+ countries. Supports 46 natural languages and 13 programming languages.

Cohere's Command model is an instruction-following LLM built for business applications. Optimized for reliable, grounded outputs in enterprise settings. First Cohere model offered via API for enterprise RAG and workflow automation.

ChatGPT reached 100 million users in 2 months — the fastest consumer product adoption in history. Fine-tuned from GPT-3.5 using RLHF, it sparked the modern AI chatbot era and changed public perception of AI.

Meta's first LLaMA came in 7B–65B sizes. The 65B model was competitive with GPT-3 175B. Its weights were leaked, spawning a massive open-source ecosystem including Alpaca, Vicuna, and dozens of derivatives.

Kosmos-1 is Microsoft's first Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM), natively processing text and images together. Capable of image captioning, visual QA, and zero-shot image classification without task-specific data.

models.architecture: Transformer (PaLM LLM + ViT Vision Encoder, embodied)
models.description: PaLM-E is the largest vision-language model at 562B parameters. Designed for embodied AI — controlling robots using natural language grounded in visual perception. Demonstrated robot planning from free-form language instructions without task-specific fine-tuning.

Jurassic-2 improved instruction-following, reduced toxicity, and expanded multilingual support to Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Italian, and Dutch. Used by enterprises via AI21 Studio for document understanding and summarization.

ChatGLM-6B is an open-source, Chinese-English bilingual dialogue model optimized for consumer-grade GPUs (as low as 6GB VRAM). Became one of the most popular open Chinese LLMs on HuggingFace. Later succeeded by ChatGLM2 and ChatGLM3.

GPT-4 demonstrated human-level performance on professional exams (bar exam top 10%, SAT 1300+). OpenAI withheld architecture details. Estimated ~1.8T parameters via MoE. First GPT with vision capabilities.
Anthropic's first Claude model built using Constitutional AI (CAI) principles. Focused on being helpful, harmless, and honest. Offered a large 100K context window compared to contemporaries.

ERNIE Bot (Wenxin Yiyan) is Baidu's ChatGPT competitor powered by ERNIE 3.5. Launched publicly on March 16, 2023 — one day after GPT-4. Reached 100M+ users rapidly. Supports Chinese multimodal tasks and integrates with Baidu Search.
Pioneered commercial text-to-video generation before competitors.
Google's answer to ChatGPT, initially powered by LaMDA then PaLM 2. Publicly launched March 21, 2023. Rebranded to Gemini in February 2024 when the underlying model switched to Gemini Pro.
Revolutionary foundation model for computer vision that automated object masking.

StableLM from Stability AI (creators of Stable Diffusion) is an open-source LLM series in 3B and 7B sizes. Trained on The Pile + additional datasets. Fine-tuned chat variants released with CC BY-SA license.
notable_features: Stability AI's text counterpart to SD

models.description: MPT-7B (MosaicPretrainedTransformer) was the first open-source 7B model to match LLaMA on benchmarks while being commercially licensed (Apache 2.0). Used ALiBi for long-context extrapolation up to 65K tokens. Training took ~9.5 days on 440 A100s.
PaLM 2 improved multilingual capabilities and reasoning. Powered Bard at launch with specialized variants including Med-PaLM 2 (medical) and Sec-PaLM (security).

models.architecture: RWKV (Receptance Weighted Key Value — linear RNN)
models.description: RWKV (pronounced 'RWaKuV') is a unique RNN architecture that achieves transformer-level performance without the quadratic attention bottleneck. Enables theoretically infinite context length with constant memory usage per token. Available from 169M to 14B parameters.

Falcon 40B from UAE's Technology Innovation Institute was trained on RefinedWeb dataset. At release it topped Hugging Face's Open LLM Leaderboard, outperforming LLaMA and MPT. Freely available commercially.

InternLM (Internal Language Model) from Shanghai AI Laboratory. Released in 7B and 20B variants. Strong coding and reasoning performance for its size. Open-source with Apache 2.0. Spawned InternLM2 with 200K context.
Trained on 'textbook-quality' data, specializing in Python coding tasks.

Kosmos-2 added spatial grounding — the ability to link phrases to bounding boxes in images. This allows referring expression comprehension and phrase grounding, key steps toward spatially-aware AI assistants.

Baichuan-13B is an open-source bilingual (Chinese-English) LLM from former Sogou CEO Wang Xiaochuan's startup. Trained on 1.4T tokens, it topped Chinese NLP leaderboards and came in base and chat variants.
Claude 2 expanded context window to 200K tokens, enabling entire books to be processed. Improved coding, reasoning, and safety. Strong competitor to GPT-4 for long-form document tasks.

Meta released LLaMA 2 in partnership with Microsoft, available in 7B, 13B, and 70B sizes. Includes chat-fine-tuned variants. Commercially available for most use cases, spawning a wave of open-source LLM products.
Major leap in composition, detail, and resolution (1024x1024).
Leading open-source vision-language model, excelling in OCR and visual grounding.
NAVER's flagship model optimized for Korean context and heavy industrial applications.
Falcon 180B was the largest open-access model at launch. Trained on 3.5T tokens from RefinedWeb. Outperformed LLaMA 2 70B and was competitive with PaLM 2 despite being fully open.

Jais 13B is the world's most capable Arabic-English bilingual LLM at launch. Developed by Inception (a G42 company) in collaboration with MBZUAI and Cornell Tech. Named after the highest peak in the UAE. Trained on 395B tokens of Arabic and English text.
notable_features: world's best Arabic LLM at release

models.description: Expanded to common sense reasoning and general knowledge while maintaining a small footprint.

Mistral 7B outperformed LLaMA 2 13B on all benchmarks despite being much smaller. Used Grouped-Query Attention and Sliding Window Attention. Released as a torrent with no press release — causing a sensation in the AI community.

DALL-E 3 significantly improved prompt adherence and image quality over DALL-E 2. Natively integrated into ChatGPT. Features automatic prompt enrichment and declines requests for images in styles of living artists.
ERNIE 4.0 is Baidu's flagship LLM powering 45M+ users via ERNIE Bot. Claimed comparable to GPT-4 at launch. Open-sourced as ERNIE 4.5 series in 2025. China's most widely used commercial LLM.
Elon Musk's xAI launched Grok 1, integrated with X (Twitter) for real-time data access. Known for its witty and less restricted responses. Grok's weights (314B MoE) were open-sourced in March 2024.
Yi-34B from Kai-Fu Lee's 01.AI topped Hugging Face's Open LLM leaderboard at launch. Bilingual Chinese-English with 200K context window. Trained on 3.1T tokens of curated multilingual data.

Google's first natively multimodal model family, available in Ultra, Pro, and Nano. Built from the ground up to understand text, images, audio, video, and code. Replaced PaLM as Google's flagship model.
Mixtral 8x7B outperformed LLaMA 2 70B and GPT-3.5 on most benchmarks. Uses only 12.9B active parameters per token despite 46.7B total, making it highly efficient. Native function calling support.
Landmark 2.7B model that matched or outperformed models 25x its size (Llama-2, Mistral 7B).
Google's flagship enterprise image generator with high-quality photorealism.
Zhipu AI's fourth-generation flagship model, rivaling GPT-4 at release. Introduced advanced retrieval and tool calling ('All Tools').
Massive open-source hit with improved alignment and multilingual support for 12+ languages.

Gemini 1.5 Pro introduced a breakthrough 1M token context window (later extended to 2M), enabling entire codebases, hour-long videos, or complete books in a single prompt. Uses efficient MoE architecture.

Gemma 1 is Google's first open-weight model family (2B and 7B), distilled from Gemini research. Outperformed LLaMA 2 13B and Mistral 7B on key benchmarks. Designed to run on laptops and consumer GPUs. Kickstarted Google's open-model strategy.
Claude 3 Sonnet balances capability and speed in the Claude 3 family. Targets enterprise workloads where both quality and throughput matter, with strong coding and reasoning.
Claude 3 Opus outperformed GPT-4 on multiple benchmarks at launch. Excels at complex analysis, research, and multi-step reasoning tasks. Most powerful model in the Claude 3 family.
Pi-2 is Inflection's second-generation model, optimized for high emotional intelligence and supportive conversation. Achieved performance parity with GPT-4 while using much less compute.
Command R (35B) is purpose-built for Retrieval-Augmented Generation workflows and multi-step tool use. Features inline citation generation for enterprise trustworthiness and supports 10 languages.
Claude 3 Haiku is the fastest and most compact model in the Claude 3 family. Designed for near-instant responsiveness, lightweight applications, and high-volume tasks while supporting vision inputs.
The 'ChatGPT moment' for AI music generation, producing full songs with vocals.
Landmark open MoE model that beat Llama 2 and Grok-1 at launch.
Improved reasoning capabilities and significantly expanded context window to 128K tokens.
Mixtral 8x22B is Mistral's largest open MoE model with 141B total (39B active) parameters. Rivals GPT-4 in reasoning and code generation. Supports function calling natively.
Suno's primary competitor, known for extremely high-fidelity audio generation.
xAI's first multimodal model, capable of processing text and visual information like diagrams and charts.
xAI's first multimodal model, bridging the gap between digital and physical worlds. Showed highly competitive spatial understanding and reasoning compared to GPT-4V and Claude 3 Opus.
China's first trillion-parameter MoE model. abab 6.5 matches GPT-4 performance levels and features a massive 200K context window.
LLaMA 3 launched in 8B and 70B sizes, with 405B following in July 2024. Trained on 15T tokens — 7x more than LLaMA 2. Significantly improved reasoning, coding, and instruction following.
Phi-3-mini (3.8B) demonstrated that small models trained on high-quality 'textbook-like' data can rival much larger models. Available in mini (3.8B), small (7B), and medium (14B) variants.
Enterprise-focused MoE model optimized for SQL generation and coding.
A large-scale foundational model built for the Indian market supporting multiple modalities and regional languages.
DeepSeek V2 introduced MLA (Multi-head Latent Attention) architecture, reducing KV-cache by 93.3%. With 236B total (21B active) parameters, it set a new efficiency benchmark for LLMs.
Expanded to all life's molecules, not just proteins (DNA, RNA, ligands).

GPT-4o ('o' for omni) natively processes text, audio, and images in real-time. Introduced near-human voice conversation with emotion detection. Matched GPT-4 Turbo quality at twice the speed and half the cost.
High-speed multimodal model optimized for efficiency and low latency with a 1M token context window.
State-of-the-art Chinese-English bilingual text-to-image model. Specifically optimized for Chinese cultural elements and characters.

Aya 23 from Cohere For AI is a state-of-the-art massively multilingual model supporting 23 languages with a focus on underrepresented languages. Built on top of Command R. Research effort involving 3,000+ contributors from 119 countries.
Mistral's first generative model specifically designed for developers, fluent in 80+ languages.
Powerful text-to-video model capable of generating up to 2-minute videos in 1080p, featuring realistic physics and complex movements.
Qwen2 surpassed LLaMA 3 on multiple benchmarks. Excellent multilingual support including Chinese, Japanese, Korean, and Arabic. The 72B model matched GPT-4o on coding tasks.
The foundation models powering 'Apple Intelligence' on consumer edge devices.
Stable Diffusion 3 uses a Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (MMDiT) architecture with dramatically improved text rendering. Available in 800M and 8B sizes. Major improvement for legible in-image text.
High-fidelity text-to-video model released to compete with Sora, capable of high-speed generation of realistic 5-second clips.
Highly efficient vision model that excels at spatial grounding, OCR, and captioning.

Claude 3.5 Sonnet set new benchmarks in coding (49% SWE-bench), reasoning, and vision. Introduced 'Artifacts' in Claude.ai. Became top-ranked on Chatbot Arena and widely praised as the best coding LLM at release.
Gemma 2 (9B and 27B) topped open-model benchmarks at launch. Features knowledge distillation from Gemini and local inference optimizations. Runs efficiently on consumer hardware and Google Colab.
A highly efficient open-weights model from Google using Gemini technology. The 27B model punched above its weight, beating Llama 3 70B on several benchmarks despite being a fraction of the size.
GPT-4o mini replaced GPT-3.5 Turbo as OpenAI's low-cost, high-performance model. Outperformed GPT-4 on some reasoning benchmarks. Accepts text and image inputs at extremely low cost.

A radically cost-efficient small model replacing GPT-3.5 Turbo. Showed remarkable multi-modal reasoning capabilities while costing significantly less, democratizing access to GPT-4 class intelligence.
notable_features: Matches GPT-4 capability at low cost
First API model supporting Safety Hierarchy

models.description: LLaMA 3.1 was the first open-source model to rival GPT-4o and Claude 3.5. Available in 8B, 70B, and 405B sizes. Trained on 15T multilingual tokens. Meta called it a milestone for open-source AI.
notable_features: first open model to rival GPT-4o

models.description: Mistral Large 2 (123B) is competitive with Claude 3.5 Sonnet and GPT-4o on coding and reasoning benchmarks. Features native function calling, 128K context, 80+ programming languages.
Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM 2) unifies real-time object segmentation in both images and videos. Capable of zero-shot transfer to any object category.
State-of-the-art text-to-image model developed by the creators of Stable Diffusion. Exceptional at text rendering and human anatomy.
Highly efficient small language model optimized for 10 major Indian languages. Breakthrough in compute-per-token efficiency for Indic NLP.
Frontier model with state-of-the-art reasoning and image generation (via Flux).
Hybrid Mamba-Transformer architecture allowing for a massive 256K context window with high efficiency.
Efficient open-source model optimized for European languages and regulatory compliance.
Mistral's first multimodal model, processing multiple images at native resolution.
A new class of models designed to spend more time thinking before they respond, excelling at complex reasoning, science, and coding.

o1-mini is a smaller, faster, cheaper version of o1. Excels at STEM reasoning and coding. Significantly outperforms GPT-4o on math benchmarks while being cost-efficient. Does not support image inputs.
Qwen2.5 improved with better instruction following, code generation, and math reasoning. Trained on 18T tokens. Specialized variants: Qwen2.5-Coder and Qwen2.5-Math.
LLaMA 3.2 introduced multimodal vision models (11B, 90B) alongside lightweight text models (1B, 3B) for edge devices. First LLaMA family to support image understanding.
Stability AI's most powerful and customizable image model. Features a massive jump in prompt adherence and image quality over SD3.
State-of-the-art Italian LLM trained from scratch on a massive Italian-centric corpus.
Anthropic's fastest model, matching the intelligence of Claude 3 Opus while being significantly cheaper and faster. Features a 200K context window.
Mistral's 124B multimodal flagship built on Mistral Large 2, outperforming GPT-4o and Gemini-1.5 Pro on document understanding benchmarks like ChartQA and DocVQA.
First major open-weight reasoning model from the Qwen team, focused on advancing AI reasoning through deep reflection and step-by-step thinking. Built on Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct.

Amazon's first major foundation model family, announced at AWS re:Invent 2024. Nova Pro is the most capable tier, balancing accuracy, speed, and cost. The family also includes Nova Lite (fast multimodal), Nova Micro (text-only low-latency), Nova Canvas (image gen), and Nova Reel (video gen).
The full version of OpenAI's o1 model, released during the '12 Days of OpenAI' event. Offers significant improvements in reasoning, accuracy, and speed over the preview version. Natively multimodal.
Sora generates realistic videos up to 1 minute from text or image prompts. Uses a diffusion transformer trained on video data. Publicly launched December 2024 after months of research preview.
Phi-4 (14B) excels at complex reasoning and STEM tasks, trained on high-quality synthetic data. Outperforms much larger models on math and coding benchmarks. MIT licensed.

Veo 2 generates high-quality videos up to 4K resolution with cinematic controls. Understands real-world physics, human motion, and complex scene transitions. Integrated into YouTube and Google's VideoFX.
DeepSeek V3 matches/beats GPT-4o at a fraction of training cost ($5.5M). First open-source GPT-4o-level model. Trained on 14.8T tokens with only 2,048 H800 GPUs using novel MLA architecture.

Mistral's updated code generation model, 2.5x faster than the original Codestral with improved architecture. Specializes in low-latency fill-in-the-middle, code completion, and test generation across 80+ languages.
A landmark model for the Indian ecosystem, optimized for 22 Indian languages.

DeepSeek R1 matched OpenAI o1 on reasoning benchmarks while being fully open-source. Its release caused a stock market reaction — Nvidia lost ~$600B market cap in one day. Includes distilled 1.5B–70B variants.
Landmark reasoning model that uses Large-Scale Reinforcement Learning to achieve performance rivaling OpenAI's o1. Known for open-sourcing the reasoning process.

o3-mini delivers o1-level reasoning at much lower cost. Supports 3 reasoning effort levels (low/medium/high). Matches o1 at medium effort. Ideal for cost-sensitive agentic workflows needing strong STEM reasoning.
The 'Flash' generation of Gemini 2.0, providing ultra-low latency multimodal reasoning and real-time audio/video capabilities.
State-of-the-art small language model optimized for reasoning-dense tasks on edge devices.

Grok 3 topped multiple reasoning benchmarks at launch. Features 'Think' mode for extended reasoning. Trained on xAI's Colossus cluster — claimed to be the world's largest GPU training cluster at launch.

Lightweight reasoning model from xAI trained on the Colossus supercluster. Features adjustable thinking effort via a reasoning_effort parameter, allowing users to balance speed vs depth. Achieves 95.8% on AIME 2024.
Anthropic's first hybrid reasoning model, allowing the model to toggle between fast responses and deep thinking mode.
Major update to the GPT-4 family with significant accuracy boosts and reduced hallucinations.

Full release of Qwen's reasoning model with 31B non-embedding parameters. Competes with o1-mini on reasoning tasks while being fully open-weight. Features function calling and structured output capabilities.
Gemma 3 is Google's open-weight model built from Gemini research. Available in 1B, 4B, 12B, and 27B. The 27B model supports vision and topped open-model Chatbot Arena at launch.
Gemini 2.5 Pro with 'Deep Think' mode topped ARC-AGI-2 and multiple reasoning benchmarks. Combines 1M token context with multimodal reasoning and strong math. Integrated into Google Workspace.

IBM Granite 3.3 is a fully open-source enterprise model under Apache 2.0. Optimized for customer service, IT automation, and cybersecurity. Available in 2B and 8B variants with guardrail and MoE versions.
LLaMA 4 Scout has a 17B active parameter MoE model (109B total) with an industry-leading 10M token context. Natively multimodal. Fits on a single H100 GPU with Int4 quantization.
LLaMA 4 Maverick has 17B active params with 128 experts (400B total). Beats GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0 Flash. Distilled from LLaMA 4 Behemoth (288B active). Best open-source multimodal model at release.

The 'teacher model' for the Llama 4 family. Released in a limited research preview, it set new records for open-weights-based intelligence but was considered too computationally expensive for general server-side release at scale.

Major API release with 21% improvement over GPT-4o on SWE-bench Verified (54.6%) and 10.5% on instruction following. Supports 1M token context. Designed for agents with superior instruction following and long-context comprehension.
Cost-efficient variant of GPT-4.1 that reduces latency by nearly half and cost by 83% compared to GPT-4o while matching or exceeding its intelligence. Supports 1M token context.
OpenAI's first nano-tier model, the fastest and cheapest in the GPT-4.1 family. Optimized for classification, autocompletion, and high-throughput tasks where speed matters most.

OpenAI o3 achieved 87.5% on ARC-AGI at high compute — a benchmark considered a near-proxy for general intelligence. Major leap in reasoning over o1. Best in class across math, coding, and science at release.
Qwen3-235B-A22B is Alibaba's flagship MoE model. Supports 119 languages. Achieves 92.3% on AIME25. Beats GPT-4o and DeepSeek-V3 on most benchmarks while being fully open-source under Apache 2.0.

Google's first fully hybrid reasoning model with configurable thinking budgets up to 32K tokens. Developers can toggle reasoning on/off. 25% faster and up to 85% cheaper per query than Gemini 1.5 Pro.
Mistral's first dedicated open-source coding agent model. Designed for agentic coding workflows including multi-file editing, codebase exploration, and autonomous software engineering tasks.
Claude 4 Sonnet introduced extended thinking with tool-use and improved memory. Optimized for complex B2B workflows, coding, and agentic tasks. Features a 1M token context window.
Claude 4 Opus is Anthropic's most powerful model for long-running agentic workflows, deep research, and complex multi-step tasks. Uses hybrid instant + extended thinking architecture.
Intermediate reasoning update to the R1 family, improving stability and reducing 'thinking loops'.
Grok 4 has a 256K context window and real-time X social media access. Competitive with frontier models on reasoning and coding. Available in Fast Reasoning and Thinking variants.
Optimized coding model from the Qwen3 family, achieving SOTA results on agentic coding benchmarks.

GPT-5 features unified intelligent routing adjusting reasoning depth per task. Claims 80% fewer hallucinations than GPT-4. Natively processes text, image, audio, video. Built-in personality customization.
GPT-5.1 expanded context to 400K tokens. Improved multi-step reasoning and long-horizon task performance. Intermediate update between GPT-5 and GPT-5.2.

Claude 4.5 Sonnet optimizes for agent stability, tool use, and explainability in production environments. Best-in-class for autonomous multi-step workflows, computer use tasks, and enterprise auditability.
Mistral Large 3 (675B MoE) delivers ~92% of GPT-5.2 performance at ~15% of the cost. Open reasoning models in 3B–14B for edge deployment. Mistral's largest and most capable model to date.
Gemini 3 Pro with Deep Think mode tops ARC-AGI-2 and AIME 2025. Extremely strong at math, reasoning, and UI-level agentic tasks. Integrated in Google's agentic platform with 2M token context.
Specialized model for breakthrough scientific research and complex logical deduction via parallel hypothesis exploration.
Claude 4.5 Opus offers significant improvements in coding and complex workplace tasks over the initial Claude 4 release.
Major efficiency update to the V3 architecture, introducing Sparse Attention for faster inference.

Mistral's frontier open-source coding model achieving 72.2% on SWE-bench Verified, the highest among open models. 123B dense transformer with 256K context, supporting function calling, tool use, and vision inputs.
Advanced Korean-centric reasoning model from Naver, integrating visual understanding with deep thinking capabilities.
GPT-5.2 is OpenAI's current flagship with a 400K context window. Achieves 100% on AIME 2025 math benchmarks. Best-in-class for multi-step reasoning, professional work, and coding as of early 2026.
Anthropic's 4.6 series top-tier model, featuring a 1M context window and market-leading performance in coding and agents.
The 'AI Director' update to Kling, featuring 4K output, native audio generation, and professional-grade cinematic controls.
Zhipu AI's flagship MoE model for early 2026. Features DeepSeek Sparse Attention and tops benchmarks in complex systems engineering.
Alibaba's native multimodal agent, combining linear attention efficiency with massive MoE knowledge.
Anthropic's 4.6 series balanced model, optimized for computer use, agent planning, and knowledge work.
Mid-sized Indic model optimized for performance-per-token, activating only 1B parameters during inference.
India's landmark 105B parameter model. Designed for complex reasoning and enterprise applications across 10+ Indic languages.
Point-version update to Gemini 3, focusing on long-horizon agentic workflows and native multimodal code generation.

First production diffusion-based reasoning LLM, generating tokens via parallel refinement rather than sequential autoregressive decoding. Achieves 1,000+ tokens/second on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, 5x faster than leading speed-optimized models.

15B open-weight multimodal reasoning model featuring hybrid THINK/NOTHINK modes for adaptive compute. Excels at math, science, GUI understanding, and vision-language tasks while requiring 10x less compute than comparable models.
notable_features: trained on 240 B200 GPUs in 4 days

models.description: 120B hybrid Mamba-Transformer MoE with only 12B active parameters per token. Features LatentMoE for 4x expert specialization, Mamba-2 layers for linear-time sequences, and native NVFP4 pretraining for 4x faster inference on Blackwell. 1M native context window.
Specialized agentic coding model for autonomous software engineering and refactoring.
Cost-efficient, high-volume model designed for speed, edge devices, and low-latency tasks.
Aimed at improving accuracy and response fluidity in everyday enterprise and user conversations.
Flagship model featuring native computer-use capabilities and a 1M context window for long-horizon agentic workflows.
A 119B open-weight Mixture-of-Experts model, unifying instruction-following, reasoning, multimodal understanding and agentic capabilities.
Smaller, budget-friendly version of GPT-5.4. Made available for Free and Go plan users.
Marketed as a 'self-evolving' model capable of recursively optimizing its own reinforcement learning harnesses. Specialized for agentic workflows.
Features a 4-agent multi-agent collaboration system allowing peer-review-style reasoning, with a 2 million token context window.
A flagship 1-trillion-parameter foundation model optimized for intelligent agent workflows, complex orchestration, and autonomous planning.
